g.text: A Comprehensive Guide to Data Structure Fundamentals

g.data-narration: A singly linked list is a linear data structure where each node contains data and a pointer to the next node only.
g.text: What is a Singly Linked List?
A linear data structure where each node contains DATA and a POINTER to the next node.
Traversal is possible in only ONE direction (forward).

g.data-narration: Each node has two fields: data stores the actual value, and next is a pointer to the following node in the list.
Insert at beginning creates a new node, points it to current head, then updates head to new node. Time O(1).
g.text: Create node → Point to head → Update head

g.data-narration: Insert at end requires traversing to the last node, then adding the new node. Time O(n) for basic implementation.
g.text: Traverse to last node → Set last->next = newNode → newNode->next = NULL

g.data-narration: Insert at position finds the node before target position and links new node. Time O(n) as it requires traversal.
g.text: Find node at position-1 → newNode->next = temp->next → temp->next = newNode

g.data-narration: Delete from beginning saves head to temp, moves head to next, then frees temp. Fastest operation O(1).
Delete from end requires finding the second-to-last node. If only one node, simply free and set head to NULL.
Delete by value searches for the node with given value. Must track previous node to update links correctly.
g.text: Search for value → Track previous node → prev->next = curr->next → free(curr)

g.data-narration: Reverse uses three pointers: prev, current, and next. We flip each link direction while traversing the list.
g.text: Three pointers: prev, current, next. Reverse each link direction iteratively.

g.data-narration: Two-pointer technique uses slow pointer moving 1 step and fast pointer moving 2 steps. When fast reaches end, slow is at middle.
g.text: 8. Find Middle Element - Two Pointer Technique
Slow pointer (1 step) + Fast pointer (2 steps). When fast reaches end, slow is at middle.
